_metadata.description: Non-standardized whole herb powders and simple botanical extracts without guaranteed marker compounds. Used for basic ingredient mapping. For premium standardized extracts with marker compounds, see standardized_botanicals.json.

functional_roles_field.purpose: Multi-valued functional role(s) for the Flutter UI tap-to-learn chip layer. Display-only — no scoring impact in V1. Most botanicals are actives (no role assigned), but some serve as colorants (turmeric), flavorings, or carriers in formulation context. Per-entry assignment in Phase 3 backfill.

_metadata.notes: This database is separate from standardized_botanicals.json by design. Overlaps are intentional and allow the scoring system to reward standardized forms over raw powders.

field_contract_changes.v5.2.0: 2026-04-30 Phase 6 — V1.1 attributes scaffolding. Added attributes object with source_origin populated on all entries (plant/fungal/algal/animal derived from category).

botanical_ingredients.notes: Euterpe oleracea (Arecaceae) fruit. High anthocyanin and polyphenol content (ORAC >15,000 µmol TE/100g). Used as antioxidant-rich superfood; freeze-dried powder retains more anthocyanins than juice. Not standardized to specific compounds.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Acai | 1 (Number of products:1141)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Malpighia emarginata (Malpighiaceae) fruit. Natural vitamin C source (1,000–4,500 mg/100g fresh weight). Often used in supplements as whole-food C source, but botanical form is not standardized to specific ascorbic acid percentage.
Pelargonium sidoides (Geraniaceae) root. EPs 7630 extract studied for acute bronchitis (PMID 18425868). Traditional Southern African remedy. Botanical form is unstandardized root powder.

Laricifomes officinalis (Fomitopsidaceae) fruiting body. Ancient polypore mushroom traditionally used for respiratory infections. Contains agaric acid and beta-glucans. Rare in the wild; some cultivated sources available.
Anacyclus pyrethrum (Asteraceae) root. Traditional Ayurvedic and Unani herb also sold as Akkalkara or Akarkara. Added for exact identity coverage.

Medicago sativa (Fabaceae) aerial parts. Traditional nutritive tonic rich in chlorophyll, vitamins K and C, and saponins. Botanical form is dried leaf powder, not standardized to specific marker compounds.

Aloe barbadensis (Asphodelaceae) leaf gel and latex. Contains acemannan polysaccharides (gel) and anthraquinone glycosides (latex). The whole-leaf and gel preparations differ markedly in composition and safety profile.

aliases: aloe vera leaves concentrated dried gel
organic aloe vera concentrated dried gel
botanical_ingredients.notes: Aloe barbadensis (Asphodelaceae) concentrated inner leaf gel. Acemannan-rich preparation with anthraquinone latex removed. Used for GI soothing and immune modulation. Not the same as whole-leaf aloe.

Fomes fomentarius (Polyporaceae) fruiting body. Tinder polypore historically used in traditional European medicine as styptic. Contains polysaccharides and triterpenes; minimal clinical evidence for supplement use.

Phyllanthus emblica (Phyllanthaceae) fruit. Ayurvedic rasayana, one of three fruits in Triphala. Exceptionally high natural vitamin C (600-1,800 mg/100g). Botanical powder form not standardized to tannin content.

Andrographis paniculata (Acanthaceae) aerial parts. 'King of Bitters' in Ayurveda and TCM. Contains andrographolides, but botanical form is raw herb powder without standardization to marker compounds.

Angelica archangelica (Apiaceae) aerial parts and root. European angelica used in traditional Western herbal medicine for digestive complaints. Contains coumarins and essential oils. Not standardized.
Angelica gigas (Apiaceae) root. Korean angelica (Cham-Dang-Gui) used in traditional Korean medicine. Contains decursin and decursinol angelate. Botanical form is unstandardized root powder.
Apocynum venetum (Apocynaceae) leaf. Venetron® is a commercial extract used for mild anxiety and sleep support. Traditional Chinese herb (Luo-Bu-Ma). Contains hyperoside and isoquercitrin flavonoids.

Processed fermentation product from apple (Malus domestica) juice. Contains acetic acid (5-6%) and trace polyphenols. Popular folk remedy but limited clinical evidence. Not a botanical per se; identity mapping for supplement labels.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Apple Cider Vinegar | 328 (Number of products:264)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Malus domestica (Rosaceae) fruit fiber. Pectin-rich dietary fiber from apple pomace. Used as prebiotic and bulking agent in supplements. Not standardized to pectin content.
Prunus armeniaca (Rosaceae) fruit. Rich in beta-carotene and dietary fiber. Used as whole-fruit powder in supplements. Kernels (not covered here) contain amygdalin and are a separate safety concern.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Apricot | 626 (Number of products:188)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ganoderma applanatum (Ganodermataceae) fruiting body. Bracket fungus related to reishi. Contains beta-glucans and triterpenes. Limited clinical research compared to G. lucidum; used in traditional medicine.
Saraca asoca (Fabaceae/Caesalpinioideae), commonly called Ashoka. Ayurvedic botanical used in women’s health formulas. Added for exact identity coverage.

Withania somnifera (Solanaceae) whole herb. Adaptogenic Ayurvedic rasayana. Contains withanolides and sitoindosides. This entry covers generic/whole-herb ashwagandha powder not standardized to specific withanolide content.

Withania somnifera (Solanaceae) root. Adaptogenic herb with RCT evidence for cortisol reduction and anxiety (PMID 23439798). Root contains higher withanolide concentration than leaf. Botanical form, not standardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Ashwagandha | 11 (Number of products:1393)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Asparagus officinalis (Asparagaceae) shoots/root. Contains steroidal saponins (shatavarin) and fructo-oligosaccharides. The Ayurvedic species Asparagus racemosus (Shatavari) is a distinct entry.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Asparagus | 1047 (Number of products:374)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Astragalus membranaceus (Fabaceae) root. Huang Qi in TCM, used as qi tonic and immune modulator. Contains astragalosides and polysaccharides. Botanical form is dried root powder, not standardized.

Atractylodes macrocephala (Asteraceae) rhizome. Bai Zhu in TCM, used for spleen-qi deficiency and digestive support. Contains atractylon and atractylenolide sesquiterpenes. Botanical form, unstandardized.
Persea americana fruit identity used for whole-fruit supplement disclosures distinct from avocado oil and avocado/soy unsaponifiables.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Avocado | 258 (Number of products:38)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Persea americana (Lauraceae) fruit oil. Cold-pressed avocado oil rich in oleic acid (50-70%), phytosterols, and fat-soluble vitamins. Used as carrier oil and nutritional supplement. Not standardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|fat|Avocado Oil | 3155 (Number of products:29)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Bacopa monnieri (Plantaginaceae) aerial parts. Brahmi in Ayurveda, used for cognitive enhancement. Contains bacosides A and B. This botanical form is whole-herb powder, not standardized to bacoside content.

Berberis vulgaris (Berberidaceae) root bark. Contains berberine alkaloid (though at lower concentration than Coptis or Phellodendron). Traditional bitter tonic for digestive and hepatobiliary support.
Hordeum vulgare (Poaceae) young grass shoots. Harvested before jointing, rich in chlorophyll, SOD enzyme, and vitamins. Alkalizing green food supplement. Not standardized to marker compounds.

Hordeum vulgare (Poaceae) grass juice powder. Concentrated form of young barley grass juice, dried at low temperature. Contains chlorophyll, enzymes, and trace minerals. More concentrated than grass powder.
Myrica cerifera (Myricaceae) root bark. Traditional astringent herb used for sore throat, diarrhea, and circulatory stimulation. Contains myricitrin flavonoid and triterpenes. Not standardized.

Beta vulgaris (Amaranthaceae) root. Rich in dietary nitrate (250-400 mg/100g), betalain pigments, and betaine. RCT evidence for exercise performance via nitric oxide pathway (PMID 28983845). Botanical powder form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Beet | 1035 (Number of products:1440)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Beta vulgaris (Amaranthaceae) root powder. Same species as beet_root entry; this covers generic 'beetroot' label variants. Rich in dietary nitrates and betalains for cardiovascular and exercise support.
Terminalia bellirica (Combretaceae) fruit. Bibhitaki in Ayurveda, one of three Triphala fruits. Contains gallic acid and ellagic acid tannins. Traditional digestive and rejuvenation remedy.
Eclipta prostrata (syn. Eclipta alba), commonly called Bhringraj. Ayurvedic herb used in liver and hair-support formulas. Added for exact identity coverage.
Vaccinium myrtillus (Ericaceae) fruit. European bilberry rich in anthocyanins (particularly delphinidin and cyanidin glycosides). Traditional eye health herb. Botanical powder form, not standardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Dwarf Bilberry | 3184 (Number of products:6)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Vaccinium myrtillus (Ericaceae) whole fruit powder. Rich in anthocyanins (300-700 mg/100g dried). Used for eye health, microcirculation, and antioxidant support. Unstandardized botanical form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Dwarf Bilberry | 3184 (Number of products:6)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Momordica charantia (Cucurbitaceae) fruit. Contains charantin and polypeptide-p with hypoglycemic properties. Traditional Ayurvedic and TCM remedy for glycemic management. Botanical form, unstandardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Bitter Melon | 350 (Number of products:244)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Prunus serotina (Rosaceae) fruit. Wild black cherry rich in anthocyanins and melatonin. Traditional use for gout and joint discomfort. Botanical fruit powder, not standardized to specific compounds.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Black cherry | 3888 (Number of products:3)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Allium sativum (Amaryllidaceae) aged/fermented bulb. Maillard reaction product with enhanced S-allyl cysteine (SAC) content compared to fresh garlic. Reduced allicin but increased antioxidant capacity.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Garlic | 4 (Number of products:1363)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Kaempferia parviflora (Zingiberaceae) rhizome. Thai black ginger (Krachai Dum). Contains polymethoxyflavones (5,7-dimethoxyflavone). RCT evidence for exercise performance and metabolic function.
Vigna mungo seed identity used for Ayurvedic masha label rows when raw DSLD classifies the ingredient under black gram.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Black Gram | 3778 (Number of products:4)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Piper nigrum (Piperaceae) fruit. Contains piperine alkaloid (5-9%) which enhances nutrient bioavailability via CYP3A4/P-gp inhibition. Whole-pepper powder form, distinct from standardized piperine extracts.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Black Pepper | 355 (Number of products:1731)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Raphanus sativus var. niger (Brassicaceae) root. Traditional European choleretic used for gallbladder and liver support. Contains glucosinolates and isothiocyanates. Botanical powder, not standardized.
Glycine max (Fabaceae) black soybean seed coat. Rich in anthocyanins (cyanidin-3-glucoside) and proanthocyanidins. Used as antioxidant and phytoestrogen source. Hull-specific preparation.

Camellia sinensis (Theaceae) fully oxidized leaf. Contains theaflavins and thearubigins (oxidized catechins) plus caffeine (30-90 mg/cup). Distinct polyphenol profile from green tea due to fermentation.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Black Tea | 1071 (Number of products:104)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ribes nigrum (Grossulariaceae) fruit. Exceptionally rich in vitamin C (180 mg/100g) and anthocyanins (250-400 mg/100g). Contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA) in seed oil. Botanical fruit powder.
Cnicus benedictus (Asteraceae) aerial parts. Traditional bitter tonic containing cnicin (sesquiterpene lactone). Used for digestive stimulation and galactagogue properties. Botanical herb powder.
Caulophyllum thalictroides (Berberidaceae) root. Contains caulosaponin and methylcytisine alkaloids. Traditional parturient herb — safety concern in pregnancy due to uterotonic and nicotinic effects.

Iris versicolor (Iridaceae) rhizome. Traditional Eclectic medicine remedy for liver and lymphatic congestion. Contains iridin glycoside. Emetic at higher doses. Botanical root powder, not standardized.

Verbena hastata (Verbenaceae) aerial parts. Native North American nervine and bitter tonic. Contains verbenalin iridoid glycoside and hastatoside. Traditional use for anxiety, tension, and liver support.
Vaccinium corymbosum (Ericaceae) fruit. Rich in anthocyanins (especially malvidin glycosides) and pterostilbene. RCT evidence for cognitive function in older adults (PMID 28283823). Botanical powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Blueberry | 23 (Number of products:1647)

audit_trail: Sprint D2.5 (2026-04-20): added 3 aliases to close silently-mapped rows surfaced by deep accuracy audit: ['blueberry juice powder', 'blueberry juice', 'organic blueberry juice powder']

botanical_ingredients.notes: Vaccinium corymbosum (Ericaceae) whole fruit powder. Contains anthocyanins, flavonols, and phenolic acids. Used for antioxidant and cognitive support. Unstandardized botanical form.
audit_trail: Sprint D2.5 (2026-04-20): added 1 aliases to close silently-mapped rows surfaced by deep accuracy audit: ['blueberry juice powder']

botanical_ingredients.notes: Peumus boldus (Monimiaceae) leaf. Chilean native tree used for hepatobiliary and digestive support. Contains boldine alkaloid and essential oil. Traditional choleretic. Botanical leaf powder.
Eupatorium perfoliatum (Asteraceae) aerial parts. Traditional Native American and Eclectic remedy for fever, colds, and flu. Contains sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. Botanical herb powder.

Boswellia serrata (Burseraceae) oleo-gum resin. Indian frankincense containing boswellic acids (AKBA, KBA). RCT evidence for joint and inflammatory support (PMID 21110389). Botanical resin, unstandardized form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Indian Frankincense | 360 (Number of products:393)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Brassaiopsis glomerulata (Araliaceae) stem bark. Vietnamese traditional medicine plant. Contains triterpene saponins. Marketed for testosterone and aromatase inhibition; very limited clinical evidence.

aliases: broccoli (brassica oleracea) floret and stalk concentrate
botanical_ingredients.notes: Brassica oleracea var. italica (Brassicaceae) floret/sprout. Rich in sulforaphane precursor (glucoraphanin) and indole-3-carbinol. Cruciferous vegetable used for detoxification support.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Broccoli | 25 (Number of products:1625)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Brassica oleracea var. gemmifera (Brassicaceae) axillary bud. Cruciferous vegetable rich in glucosinolates (sinigrin, glucobrassicin) and vitamin K. Used in whole-food supplement blends.
audit_trail: Sprint D2.5 (2026-04-20): added 1 aliases to close silently-mapped rows surfaced by deep accuracy audit: ['brussels']

botanical_ingredients.notes: Agathosma betulina (Rutaceae) leaf. South African traditional medicine for urinary tract infections and inflammation. Contains diosphenol and pulegone terpenoids. Botanical leaf powder.
Rhamnus cathartica (Rhamnaceae) bark. Stimulant laxative containing anthraquinone glycosides (emodin, chrysophanol). Traditional use for constipation. Not for long-term use. Botanical bark powder.
Fagopyrum esculentum (Polygonaceae) seed. Pseudocereal rich in rutin (quercetin-3-rutinoside), resistant starch, and D-chiro-inositol. Gluten-free grain alternative with cardiovascular benefits.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Buckwheat | 365 (Number of products:104)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Bulbine natalensis (Asphodelaceae) root/stem. South African succulent marketed for testosterone support. Contains mesembrenone alkaloids and saponins. Limited human clinical evidence; mostly animal studies.
Bupleurum chinense (Apiaceae) root. Chai Hu in TCM, one of the most commonly prescribed herbs. Contains saikosaponins (a, c, d). Used for liver qi stagnation and as harmonizing herb.
Arctium lappa (Asteraceae) root. Contains inulin-type fructans (up to 50% dry weight), arctigenin lignan, and chlorogenic acid. Traditional blood purifier and prebiotic. Botanical root powder.
Ruscus aculeatus (Asparagaceae) rhizome. Contains ruscogenin and neoruscogenin steroidal saponins. European HMPC-approved for chronic venous insufficiency symptoms. Botanical root form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Butcher's Broom | 1676 (Number of products:107)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Juglans cinerea (Juglandaceae) inner bark. Traditional Eclectic medicine laxative containing juglone and juglandin. Gentle cathartic used for constipation and liver support. Botanical bark powder.
Brassica oleracea var. capitata (Brassicaceae) leaf. Cruciferous vegetable rich in glucosinolates (sinigrin), vitamin C, and vitamin K. Used in whole-food supplement blends for detoxification.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Cabbage | 183 (Number of products:722)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Theobroma cacao (Malvaceae) seed powder. Rich in flavanols (epicatechin, catechin), theobromine, and magnesium. RCT evidence for cardiovascular benefits and blood pressure (PMID 22301923). Unprocessed form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|other|Chocolate | 3008 (Number of products:4)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Eschscholzia californica (Papaveraceae) aerial parts. Contains protopine and californidine alkaloids. Non-opiate sedative with GABA receptor affinity. French HMPC-approved for mild anxiety and insomnia.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|California Poppy | 180 (Number of products:74)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Caralluma fimbriata (Apocynaceae) aerial parts. Indian succulent used in Ayurveda for appetite suppression. Contains pregnane glycosides. RCT evidence for appetite control (PMID 17097761). Botanical powder.

Caralluma fimbriata (Apocynaceae) aerial parts. Same species as caralluma entry; covers specific label variants. Contains pregnane glycosides used traditionally as famine food and appetite suppressant.
Elettaria cardamomum (Zingiberaceae) seed. Contains 1,8-cineole and alpha-terpinyl acetate in essential oil. Traditional carminative spice in Ayurveda and Unani medicine. Botanical seed powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Cardamom | 43 (Number of products:264)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ceratonia siliqua (Fabaceae) pod. Contains galactomannan gum (locust bean gum), tannins, and D-pinitol. Traditional Mediterranean food used in supplements as thickener and mild antidiarrheal.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Carob | 1175 (Number of products:53)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Daucus carota subsp. sativus (Apiaceae) root. Rich in beta-carotene (provitamin A, 8-13 mg/100g), polyacetylenes (falcarinol), and dietary fiber. Used in whole-food supplement blends.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Carrot | 373 (Number of products:1291)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Daucus carota (Apiaceae) seed-derived oil. Preserve as a distinct carrot seed oil identity when the label declares the oil itself rather than carrot root, carrot extract, or beta-carotene.
Manihot esculenta (Euphorbiaceae) root. Starchy tropical tuber; source of tapioca. Contains linamarin (cyanogenic glycoside) in raw form. Used in supplements as excipient starch source.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Cassava | 1700 (Number of products:12)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Cassia mimosoides var. nomame (Fabaceae) aerial parts. Japanese traditional herb containing flavan dimers with lipase-inhibiting activity. Used in weight management supplements. Limited human clinical data.
Uncaria tomentosa (Rubiaceae) inner bark. Uña de Gato from Peruvian Amazon. Contains pentacyclic oxindole alkaloids (POAs). Used for immune modulation and joint inflammation. Botanical bark form.

Nepeta cataria (Lamiaceae) aerial parts. Contains nepetalactone (iridoid monoterpene). Traditional nervine and carminative. Used for mild anxiety, insomnia, and digestive complaints. Botanical leaf powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Catnip | 1701 (Number of products:77)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Trichilia catigua (Meliaceae) bark. Brazilian traditional aphrodisiac and nervine tonic. Note: 'catuaba' refers to multiple unrelated species (Erythroxylum, Anemopaegma). This entry covers Trichilia catigua bark.

Brassica oleracea var. botrytis (Brassicaceae) inflorescence. Cruciferous vegetable rich in glucosinolates (glucobrassicin), vitamin C, and sulforaphane precursors. Used in whole-food supplement blends.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Cauliflower | 458 (Number of products:492)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Capsicum annuum (Solanaceae) fruit. Contains capsaicin (8-methyl-N-vanillyl-6-nonenamide) at 0.1-1% in cayenne varieties. Thermogenic and circulatory stimulant. Botanical powder, not standardized to capsaicin.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Cayenne Pepper | 29 (Number of products:346)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Celastrus paniculatus (Celastraceae) seed. Jyotishmati in Ayurveda, traditional nootropic and nerve tonic. Seed oil contains sesquiterpenes and polyesters. Limited clinical evidence for cognitive enhancement.

Apium graveolens (Apiaceae) seed. Contains 3-n-butylphthalide (NBP) and apigenin. Traditional remedy for gout and joint pain. Also contains furanocoumarins — photosensitivity possible. Botanical seed form.

Cinnamomum verum (Lauraceae) bark. 'True cinnamon' with lower coumarin content (~0.004%) than Cassia cinnamon. Preferred for long-term supplementation due to hepatotoxicity risk of high-coumarin Cassia.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Cinnamon | 1578 (Number of products:1035)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Inonotus obliquus (Hymenochaetaceae) sclerotium. Parasitic birch fungus rich in betulinic acid, melanin, and beta-glucans. Traditional Siberian and Scandinavian remedy. High ORAC value. Botanical powder form.
Matricaria chamomilla (Asteraceae) flower. Contains apigenin, bisabolol, and chamazulene. RCT evidence for mild anxiolytic and sleep-promoting effects (PMID 27912875). Most-studied calming botanical.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Chamomile (unspecified) | 2257 (Number of products:206)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Phyllanthus niruri (Phyllanthaceae) aerial parts. 'Stone breaker' used in Ayurveda and South American folk medicine for kidney stones and liver support. Contains lignans (phyllanthin, hypophyllanthin).
Vitex agnus-castus (Lamiaceae) berry. Contains diterpenes and iridoids acting on dopamine D2 receptors. European HMPC-approved for PMS symptoms. RCT evidence for menstrual regularity (PMID 23136064).
Terminalia chebula (Combretaceae) fruit. Haritaki in Ayurveda, one of three Triphala fruits. Rich in chebulic acid, gallic acid, and ellagic acid. Traditional rejuvenation and digestive remedy.
Rosa laevigata fruit botanical identity. Used in traditional formulations and appears in supplement labels as Cherokee Rose fruit extract preparations.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Cherokee Rosehip | 1967 (Number of products:1)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Salvia hispanica (Lamiaceae) seed. Rich in alpha-linolenic acid (ALA, 17-20%), soluble fiber (mucilage), and complete protein. Aztec superfood. Botanical seed powder, not standardized.
Stellaria media (Caryophyllaceae) aerial parts. Traditional Western herbal remedy for skin conditions and mild inflammation. Contains saponins, coumarins, and flavonoids. Botanical herb powder.
Cichorium intybus (Asteraceae) root. Rich in inulin (35-45% dry weight), a prebiotic fructo-oligosaccharide. Also contains sesquiterpene lactones (lactucin). Used for prebiotic and hepatic support.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|chicory | 1196 (Number of products:120)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Capsicum annuum (Solanaceae) fruit. Contains capsaicinoids (capsaicin, dihydrocapsaicin) with thermogenic and analgesic properties. Covers generic chili/hot pepper label variants. Botanical powder form.
Cuscuta chinensis (Convolvulaceae) seed. Tu Si Zi in TCM, used for kidney yang and liver-kidney deficiency patterns. Contains flavonoids (kaempferol, quercetin) and lignans. Parasitic plant seed.

Glycyrrhiza uralensis (Fabaceae) root. Gan Cao in TCM, most frequently used herb in Chinese formulas. Contains glycyrrhizin (50x sweeter than sucrose) and liquiritigenin flavonoid. Botanical root form.

Chlorella vulgaris (Chlorellaceae) whole cell. Green microalga rich in chlorophyll, protein (50-60%), and Chlorella Growth Factor (CGF). Cell wall must be broken for bioavailability. Botanical algae powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Chlorella | 36 (Number of products:1085)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Aronia melanocarpa (Rosaceae) fruit. Among the highest anthocyanin content of any berry (1,480 mg/100g). Rich in proanthocyanidins and chlorogenic acid. Potent antioxidant. Botanical fruit powder.

Coriandrum sativum (Apiaceae) leaf (herb). Same plant as coriander (seed). Traditionally used for heavy metal chelation support (limited evidence). Contains linalool and dodecenal. Botanical leaf powder.
Cinnamomum cassia (Lauraceae) bark. Chinese/Cassia cinnamon with higher coumarin content (1%) than Ceylon cinnamon. Contains cinnamaldehyde (65-80% of essential oil). Botanical bark powder.
Cissus sicyoides leaf, commonly marketed as Insulina. Added for exact identity coverage from raw DSLD label text.
Citrus bergamia (Rutaceae) fruit. Southern Italian citrus rich in flavanones (neoeriocitrin, naringin, neohesperidin). RCT evidence for lipid management (PMID 26268692). Botanical fruit powder.

Generic citrus (Rutaceae) fruit extract covering multiple Citrus species. Contains bioflavonoids (hesperidin, naringin, rutin). Identity mapping for non-specific citrus extract labels. Not standardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Citrus Bioflavonoids | 21 (Number of products:165)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Galium aparine (Rubiaceae) aerial parts. Traditional Western lymphatic and diuretic herb. Contains iridoid glycosides (asperuloside), coumarins, and flavonoids. Eclectic medicine remedy for edema.
Syzygium aromaticum (Myrtaceae) flower bud. Contains eugenol (72-90% of essential oil), beta-caryophyllene, and acetyl eugenol. Powerful antimicrobial and analgesic spice. Botanical whole-bud form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Clove | 2167 (Number of products:289)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Cnidium monnieri (Apiaceae) seed (fruit). She Chuang Zi in TCM. Contains osthole coumarin with anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. Traditional use for skin conditions and libido support.
Codonopsis pilosula (Campanulaceae) root. Dang Shen in TCM, used as a gentler substitute for Panax ginseng. Contains codonopilate saponins and polysaccharides. Qi-tonifying adaptogenic herb.

aliases: green coffee bean coffea robusta extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Coffea arabica (Rubiaceae) whole fruit (cherry). Contains chlorogenic acids and polyphenols distinct from coffee bean. NeuroFactor® studies show BDNF elevation from coffee fruit extract. Botanical fruit form.
Coleus forskohlii (Lamiaceae) root. Contains forskolin diterpene that directly activates adenylate cyclase/cAMP pathway. RCT evidence for body composition (PMID 15947455). Botanical root form.

Vitis labrusca (Vitaceae) fruit. North American grape variety rich in anthocyanins (malvidin, delphinidin) and resveratrol. Higher polyphenol content than most European grape varieties. Botanical fruit powder.
Coptis chinensis (Ranunculaceae) rhizome. Huang Lian in TCM, one of the richest natural sources of berberine alkaloid (5-8%). Bitter cold herb used for heat-clearing and antimicrobial purposes.

aliases: organic cordyceps (cordyceps militaris) fruiting body extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Cordyceps militaris (Cordycipitaceae) fruiting body or mycelium. Contains cordycepin (3'-deoxyadenosine) and beta-glucans. Traditional Tibetan/Chinese tonic for endurance and kidney yang. Botanical powder.

Cordyceps militaris (Cordycipitaceae) fruiting body powder. Same species as cordyceps entry; covers powder-specific label variants. Contains cordycepin and adenosine. Cultivated form (wild C. sinensis is rare).

Coriandrum sativum (Apiaceae) seed. Contains linalool (60-70% of essential oil) and monoterpenes. Traditional carminative and digestive spice. Distinct from cilantro (leaf of same plant). Botanical seed form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Coriander | 1205 (Number of products:264)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Corydalis yanhusuo root, a traditional East Asian botanical used in pain-support formulas. Added for exact Softgels label coverage.
Elymus repens (syn. Agropyron repens, Poaceae) rhizome. Traditional European diuretic and demulcent. Contains triticin polysaccharide and mucilage. Used for urinary tract inflammation.
Primula veris (Primulaceae) flower. European traditional expectorant containing saponins (primulasaponin) and flavonoids. Component of Sinupret® for sinusitis. Botanical flower preparation.
Viburnum opulus (Adoxaceae) bark. Contains viopudial and scopoletin with smooth muscle antispasmodic activity. Traditional remedy for menstrual cramps and muscle spasms. Botanical bark powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Cramp Bark | 462 (Number of products:75)

aliases: Pacran SP Cranberry Vaccinium macrocarpon powder
botanical_ingredients.notes: Vaccinium macrocarpon (Ericaceae) fruit. Contains type-A proanthocyanidins (PACs) with anti-adhesion activity against E. coli uropathogenic strains. RCT evidence for UTI prevention (PMID 23440782).

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|cranberry | 47 (Number of products:1736)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Vaccinium macrocarpon (Ericaceae) whole fruit powder. Same species as cranberry entry; covers fruit-specific labels. Rich in type-A proanthocyanidins and hippuric acid. Botanical fruit form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|cranberry | 47 (Number of products:1736)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Geranium maculatum (Geraniaceae) root. Traditional North American astringent herb rich in tannins (up to 30%). Used for diarrhea, hemorrhoids, and mucosal inflammation. Botanical root powder.
Cucumis sativus (Cucurbitaceae) fruit. Contains cucurbitacins, lignans, and flavonoids. High water content (95%). Used in supplements for hydration and as silica source. Botanical fruit powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|cucumber | 1325 (Number of products:240)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Cuminum cyminum (Apiaceae) seed. Contains cuminaldehyde and thymoquinone. Traditional carminative and digestive spice in Ayurveda and Middle Eastern medicine. Botanical seed powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Cumin | 17 (Number of products:91)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Acacia catechu (Fabaceae) heartwood. Contains catechin and epicatechin flavonoids. Traditional Ayurvedic astringent (Khadira) used for oral health and skin conditions. Botanical heartwood extract.
Cyperus rotundus (Cyperaceae) rhizome. Xiang Fu in TCM, Musta in Ayurveda. Contains sesquiterpenes (cyperotundone, alpha-cyperone). Used for menstrual regulation and digestive stagnation.
Taraxacum officinale (Asteraceae) whole plant. Contains taraxasterol triterpene, chicoric acid, and inulin. Traditional hepatic, diuretic, and digestive bitter herb. Botanical whole-plant powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Dandelion | 123 (Number of products:1460)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Taraxacum officinale (Asteraceae) root. Root-specific preparation rich in inulin (up to 40% in autumn harvest) and sesquiterpene lactones. Traditional choleretic and prebiotic. Botanical root powder.

Salvia miltiorrhiza (Lamiaceae) root. Dan Shen in TCM, primary blood-moving herb. Contains tanshinones (lipophilic) and salvianolic acids (hydrophilic). Drug interaction risk with warfarin. Botanical root form.
Prunus avium (Rosaceae) fruit. Sweet cherry variety rich in anthocyanins (cyanidin-3-rutinoside), melatonin, and phenolic acids. Used for gout, sleep support, and post-exercise recovery.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Sweet Cherry | 55 (Number of products:287)

audit_trail: Sprint D2.5 (2026-04-20): added 2 aliases to close silently-mapped rows surfaced by deep accuracy audit: ['cherry puree powder', 'sweet cherry powder']

botanical_ingredients.notes: Phoenix dactylifera (Arecaceae) fruit. Rich in natural sugars (glucose, fructose), potassium, and phenolic compounds. Traditional Middle Eastern energy food. Used in supplement blends as natural sweetener.

aliases: devil’s claw (harpagophytum procumbens and harpagophytum zeyheri) extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Harpagophytum procumbens (Pedaliaceae) secondary root tuber. Contains harpagoside iridoid glycoside. European HMPC-approved for musculoskeletal pain. RCT evidence for osteoarthritis (PMID 14669258).
Glycyrrhiza glabra (Fabaceae) root with glycyrrhizin removed (<3%). DGL retains flavonoids while eliminating aldosterone-mimetic effects. Used for gastric mucosal protection and peptic ulcer support.
Anethum graveolens (Apiaceae) aerial parts. Contains carvone and limonene monoterpenes. Traditional carminative and digestive herb. Used in supplements for mild GI complaints. Botanical herb powder.

Angelica sinensis (Apiaceae) root. Dang Gui in TCM, primary blood-tonifying herb. Contains Z-ligustilide and ferulic acid. Used for menstrual regulation and blood deficiency patterns. Botanical root form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Dong Quai | 58 (Number of products:647)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Palmaria palmata (Palmariaceae) thallus. North Atlantic red seaweed rich in protein (up to 25%), iron, potassium, and iodine. Traditional Irish and Icelandic food. Botanical seaweed powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Dulse | 710 (Number of products:203)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Echinacea angustifolia (Asteraceae) root. Narrow-leaved coneflower, traditional Plains Indian immune herb. Contains echinacoside and alkylamides. Botanical root form, not standardized to marker compounds.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Echinacea angustifolia | 2259 (Number of products:317)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Echinacea purpurea (Asteraceae) aerial parts. Purple coneflower above-ground portion rich in cichoric acid and polysaccharides. RCT evidence for upper respiratory infection duration (PMID 17597571).
Echinacea purpurea (Asteraceae) whole herb. Covers generic echinacea herb labels. Contains alkylamides and cichoric acid. Botanical whole-herb form distinct from root-only preparations.

Echinacea purpurea (Asteraceae) root extract. Root portion contains higher alkylamide concentration than aerial parts. Traditional immune tonic. Botanical extract, not standardized to marker compounds.
Sambucus nigra (Adoxaceae) flower. Elder flower contains flavonoids (rutin, isoquercitrin) and triterpenes. European HMPC-approved for common cold symptoms. Traditional diaphoretic and febrifuge.

aliases: Black Elder (Sambucus nigra) berry juice powder
organic black elder (sambucus nigra l.) extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Sambucus nigra (Adoxaceae) fruit. Rich in anthocyanins (cyanidin-3-sambubioside, cyanidin-3-glucoside). RCT evidence for influenza duration reduction (PMID 15080016). Most studied berry for immune support.
Eleutherococcus senticosus (Araliaceae) stem/root bark. Siberian ginseng (not true Panax). Contains eleutheroside B (syringin) and eleutheroside E. Adaptogenic herb for stress resilience.

Flammulina velutipes (Physalacriaceae) fruiting body. Enoki mushroom containing flammulin protein and beta-glucans. Used in functional food and mushroom supplement blends. Minimal clinical supplement data.
Multi-species essential oil blend covering generic essential oil supplement labels. Composition varies by product. Identity mapping only; not attributable to a single botanical species.
Eucalyptus globulus (Myrtaceae) leaf. Contains 1,8-cineole (eucalyptol, 60-85% of essential oil). Traditional respiratory herb. Used as inhalant and in supplements for sinus and bronchial support.
Eucalyptus globulus (Myrtaceae) leaf essential oil. Standardized to 1,8-cineole content by essential oil standards but botanical-grade supplement form. Used for respiratory and antimicrobial support.
Fraxinus excelsior (Oleaceae) seed/fruit (samara). Contains secoiridoids (oleuropein analogs) and hydroxycoumarins. European traditional remedy for joint health. Botanical seed/fruit form.

aliases: cold pressed oil of evening primrose
evening primrose oil oenothera biennis seed supercritical extract
evening primrose oil (oenothera biennis) (seed) supercritical extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Oenothera biennis (Onagraceae) seed oil. Rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA, 7-14%) and linoleic acid. RCT evidence for eczema/atopic dermatitis (Cochrane review). Botanical seed oil form.
Tetradium ruticarpum (syn. Evodia rutaecarpa, Rutaceae) fruit. Wu Zhu Yu in TCM. Contains evodiamine and rutaecarpine alkaloids. Traditional warming herb for digestive and headache complaints.
Euphrasia officinalis (Orobanchaceae) aerial parts. Traditional European herb for eye inflammation and hay fever. Contains aucubin iridoid, caffeic acid, and flavonoids. Limited clinical evidence.

Chamaelirium luteum (Melanthiaceae) root. Contains chamaelirin (helonin) steroidal saponin. Traditional Eclectic medicine remedy for female reproductive complaints. Conservation concern due to overharvesting.
Foeniculum vulgare (Apiaceae) seed. Contains trans-anethole (50-80% of essential oil) and fenchone. Traditional carminative and galactagogue. RCT evidence for infantile colic (PMID 12868253).

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Fennel | 66 (Number of products:703)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Trigonella foenum-graecum (Fabaceae) seed. Contains 4-hydroxyisoleucine and galactomannan fiber (50%). RCT evidence for glycemic control and testosterone (PMID 20676249). Botanical seed powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Fenugreek | 253 (Number of products:851)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Tanacetum parthenium (Asteraceae) leaf. Contains parthenolide sesquiterpene lactone. European HMPC-approved for migraine prophylaxis. RCT evidence (PMID 15257711). Botanical leaf form, unstandardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Feverfew | 436 (Number of products:132)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ficus carica (Moraceae) fruit. Contains ficin enzyme, phenolic acids, and dietary fiber. Traditional laxative and nutritive food. Used in supplement blends. Botanical dried fruit powder.
Forsythia suspensa (Oleaceae) fruit. Lian Qiao in TCM, paired with honeysuckle (Yin Qiao San). Contains forsythiaside and phillyrin lignans. Heat-clearing and detoxifying herb.
Angelica dahurica (Apiaceae) root. Also called fragrant angelica or Bai Zhi in traditional East Asian medicine. Added for exact identity coverage of Softgels label variants.
Rhamnus frangula (syn. Frangula alnus, Rhamnaceae) bark. Contains anthraquinone glycosides (glucofrangulins A & B). Stimulant laxative. Must be aged 1 year before use to reduce emodin content.

Cucumis melo (Cucurbitaceae) fruit. SOD-rich melon variety (Cucumis melo LC) from southern France. Source of plant-derived superoxide dismutase. Used in antioxidant supplements. Botanical fruit extract.
Chionanthus virginicus (Oleaceae) bark. Traditional Eclectic medicine remedy for liver and gallbladder complaints. Contains phyllyrin and saponins. Gentle hepatic alterative. Botanical bark powder.
Generic multi-fruit powder blend used as supplement base or flavoring. Composition varies by manufacturer. Identity mapping for non-specific fruit base labels. Not attributable to a single species.
Generic multi-fruit blend covering non-specific 'fruits' label terms in supplements. Identity mapping only; species composition varies by product. Not standardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Pineapple | 193 (Number of products:508)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Galega officinalis (Fabaceae) aerial parts. French Lilac / Goat's Rue. Historical source of metformin's development (contains galegine, a guanidine derivative). Traditional galactagogue.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Goat's rue | 3819 (Number of products:4)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Garcinia indica (Clusiaceae) fruit rind. Kokum fruit containing garcinol and hydroxycitric acid (HCA, lower than G. cambogia). Traditional Indian Ayurvedic digestive aid. Botanical rind extract.
Gardenia jasminoides (Rubiaceae) fruit. Zhi Zi in TCM, heat-clearing herb. Contains geniposide and crocin (yellow pigment). Used for liver heat patterns and inflammation. Botanical fruit powder.

Allium sativum (Amaryllidaceae) fresh/dried bulb. Contains alliin → allicin (via alliinase on crushing). RCT evidence for blood pressure and lipids (PMID 23590705). Botanical bulb form, unstandardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Garlic | 4 (Number of products:1363)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Pelargonium graveolens (Geraniaceae) aerial parts. Rose geranium used in aromatherapy and herbal preparations. Contains citronellol and geraniol monoterpenes. Botanical herb powder, not standardized.
Zingiber officinale (Zingiberaceae) rhizome extract. Contains gingerols and shogaols. RCT evidence for nausea/vomiting (PMID 10793599). Extract form more concentrated than powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Ginger | 70 (Number of products:3297)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Zingiber officinale (Zingiberaceae) rhizome. Contains 6-gingerol (primary bioactive), 8-gingerol, 10-gingerol, and 6-shogaol. Warming carminative and anti-emetic. Botanical root powder form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Ginger | 70 (Number of products:3297)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ginkgo biloba (Ginkgoaceae) leaf. Contains flavone glycosides (quercetin, kaempferol) and terpene lactones (ginkgolides, bilobalide). Botanical leaf form, distinct from standardized EGb 761® extract.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Ginkgo | 71 (Number of products:1552)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Panax ginseng (Araliaceae) root. Asian/Korean ginseng containing ginsenosides (Rb1, Rg1, Rg3). Most-studied adaptogenic herb. Botanical root form, not standardized to specific ginsenoside content.
Gleditsia sinensis (Fabaceae) thorn/fruit. Zao Jiao in TCM. Contains gleditsia saponins. Traditional phlegm-transforming herb. Used for respiratory congestion and abscesses. Botanical form.
Glehnia littoralis (Apiaceae) root. Bei Sha Shen in TCM. Contains coumarins (imperatorin) and polyacetylenes. Yin-nourishing herb for lung dryness and stomach yin deficiency.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Glehnia root | 2835 (Number of products:13)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Cynara scolymus (Asteraceae) leaf. Contains cynarin and chlorogenic acid. European HMPC-approved for dyspepsia. RCT evidence for lipid reduction (PMID 18424099). Botanical leaf preparation.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Artichoke | 330 (Number of products:521)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Galega officinalis (Fabaceae) aerial parts. Same as galega_officinalis — Goat's Rue. Traditional galactagogue. Contains galegine guanidine derivative. Botanical herb form. See also galega entry.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Goat's rue | 3819 (Number of products:4)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Lycium barbarum (Solanaceae) fruit. Gou Qi Zi in TCM. Rich in zeaxanthin, Lycium barbarum polysaccharides (LBPs), and betaine. Traditional liver-kidney tonic. Botanical dried fruit powder.
Solidago virgaurea (Asteraceae) aerial parts. European goldenrod. Contains saponins (virgaureasaponins), flavonoids, and leiocarposide. European HMPC-approved as urinary tract anti-inflammatory.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Solidago | 3057 (Number of products:1)

aliases: goldenseal (hydrastis canadensis) root and rhizome extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Hydrastis canadensis (Ranunculaceae) root/rhizome. Contains berberine (2-4%) and hydrastine alkaloids. Traditional Eclectic medicine antimicrobial and mucous membrane tonic. Conservation concern (CITES Appendix II).

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Goldenseal | 78 (Number of products:580)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Centella asiatica (Apiaceae) aerial parts. Contains asiaticoside, madecassoside, and triterpene acids. Ayurvedic medhya rasayana (brain tonic). RCT evidence for wound healing and venous insufficiency.

aliases: french grape vitis vinifera seed extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Vitis vinifera (Vitaceae) fruit. Contains resveratrol, quercetin, and anthocyanins (in red varieties). Botanical whole-grape powder form covering generic grape labels. Not standardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Grape | 80 (Number of products:2825)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Vitis vinifera (Vitaceae) seed. Rich in oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs, 80-95%). RCT evidence for blood pressure and endothelial function (PMID 21802563). Botanical seed form, unstandardized.

Eutrochium purpureum (syn. Eupatorium purpureum, Asteraceae) root. Traditional Eclectic medicine remedy for kidney stones and urinary complaints. Contains euparin and flavonoids. Botanical root powder.
Annona muricata (Annonaceae) leaf. Soursop/graviola containing annonaceous acetogenins. Neurotoxicity concern from annonacin (atypical parkinsonism, PMID 15269786). Traditional tropical remedy.

Chelidonium majus (Papaveraceae) aerial parts. Greater celandine containing chelidonine and sanguinarine alkaloids. Traditional European choleretic. Hepatotoxicity cases reported (EMA/HMPC assessment). Use with caution.
Alpinia galanga rhizome, commonly called greater galangal or galanga. Added for exact identity coverage from raw DSLD label text.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Greater galangal | 3642 (Number of products:6)

aliases: greek mountain tea aerial parts extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Sideritis scardica (Lamiaceae) aerial parts. Greek/Balkan mountain tea rich in flavonoids and diterpenes. Traditional remedy for colds, respiratory complaints, and digestive support. Emerging cognitive research.
Capsicum annuum (Solanaceae) unripe fruit. Sweet pepper variety (no capsaicin). Rich in vitamin C and chlorophyll. Used in whole-food supplement blends for nutrient density.
Brassica oleracea var. capitata (Brassicaceae) green leaf. Cruciferous vegetable rich in glucosinolates, vitamin K, and S-methylmethionine (vitamin U). Used in whole-food supplement blends.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Cabbage | 183 (Number of products:722)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Allium fistulosum (Amaryllidaceae) leaf/bulb. Scallion/spring onion containing organosulfur compounds and quercetin. Used in whole-food supplement blends for antioxidant content.
Camellia sinensis (Theaceae) unoxidized leaf. Contains epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG, 25-40% of catechins) and L-theanine. Extensive RCT evidence for metabolic and cardiovascular benefits.

aliases: organic green tea (camellia sinensis) (leaf) aqueous extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Camellia sinensis (Theaceae) whole unoxidized leaf. Same species as green_tea; covers leaf-specific labels. Contains catechins (EGCG), caffeine, and L-theanine. Botanical leaf powder form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Green Tea | 1173 (Number of products:3408)

aliases: organic Guava fruit and leaf (Psidium guajava) extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Psidium guajava (Myrtaceae) fruit/leaf. Tropical fruit exceptionally rich in vitamin C (228 mg/100g). Leaf contains quercetin and gallic acid. RCT evidence for glycemic control (PMID 20170471).

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Guava | 1437 (Number of products:145)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ilex guayusa (Aquifoliaceae) leaf. Amazonian holly related to yerba maté. Contains caffeine (1.7-3.5%), chlorogenic acids, and L-theanine. Traditional Ecuadorian stimulant tea. Botanical leaf form.
Commiphora wightii (Burseraceae) oleo-gum resin. Ayurvedic guggulu containing guggulsterones (E and Z forms). Traditional remedy for hyperlipidemia and arthritis. Botanical resin, unstandardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Guggul | 1588 (Number of products:324)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Gymnema sylvestre (Apocynaceae) leaf. Gurmar ('sugar destroyer') in Ayurveda. Contains gymnemic acids that block sweet taste receptors. RCT evidence for glycemic control (PMID 11370345).

Gynostemma pentaphyllum (Cucurbitaceae) leaf. Jiaogulan, 'Southern ginseng.' Contains gypenosides (saponins structurally similar to ginsenosides). Adaptogenic and AMPK-activating. Botanical leaf form.

Crataegus monogyna (Rosaceae) flowering tops (leaf, flower, berry). Contains vitexin, hyperoside, and OPCs. European HMPC-approved for mild heart failure support (NYHA I-II). Well-studied cardiotonic.
Hyoscyamus niger seed identity used for khurasani ajwain label rows in Ayurvedic products.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Henbane | 1996 (Number of products:6)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Flavanone glycoside found primarily in citrus peels (Citrus sinensis, C. reticulata). Not a botanical per se but a plant-derived bioflavonoid. Used for venous tone and capillary integrity support.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Hesperidin | 83 (Number of products:829)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Agaricus blazei (Agaricaceae) fruiting body. Royal Sun agaricus mushroom rich in beta-glucans (beta-1,6-D-glucan). Japanese health food (Himematsutake). Immune-modulating properties. Botanical mushroom powder.
Pleurotus ostreatus (Pleurotaceae) fruiting body. Oyster mushroom containing pleuran beta-glucan, lovastatin (trace), and ergothioneine. Culinary mushroom with immune-modulating properties.

aliases: holy basil ocimum sanctum leaf supercritical extract
holy basil ocimum sanctum leaf steam-distilled extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Ocimum tenuiflorum (syn. O. sanctum, Lamiaceae) leaf. Tulsi in Ayurveda, adaptogenic sacred herb. Contains eugenol, rosmarinic acid, and ursolic acid. RCT evidence for stress and glycemic control.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Holy Basil | 2220 (Number of products:489)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Hoodia gordonii (Apocynaceae) stem. Southern African succulent used by San people as appetite suppressant. Contains P57 steroidal glycoside. CITES-listed. Clinical trials showed modest effects; safety concerns.
Marrubium vulgare (Lamiaceae) aerial parts. White horehound containing marrubiin diterpene lactone. European HMPC-approved for productive cough and dyspepsia. Traditional expectorant herb.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Horehound | 1226 (Number of products:88)

aliases: horny goat weed aerial parts extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Epimedium spp. (Berberidaceae) aerial parts. Yin Yang Huo in TCM. Contains icariin prenylated flavonol with PDE5-inhibitory activity. Traditional kidney yang tonic. Several Epimedium species used.

Aesculus hippocastanum (Sapindaceae) seed. Contains aescin (escin) triterpene saponin mixture. European HMPC-approved for chronic venous insufficiency. RCT evidence (PMID 22592684). Botanical seed form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Horse Chestnut | 1223 (Number of products:144)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Mentha longifolia leaf botanical identity used in supplement labels as horsemint leaf extract.
Armoracia rusticana (Brassicaceae) root. Contains sinigrin glucosinolate → allyl isothiocyanate (pungent). Traditional antimicrobial and sinus-clearing herb. Botanical root powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Horseradish | 1222 (Number of products:77)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Equisetum arvense (Equisetaceae) aerial parts. Richest botanical source of bioavailable silica (silicon dioxide). Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids. Traditional diuretic and connective tissue herb.
Genipa americana (Rubiaceae) fruit. Amazonian fruit rich in genipin and iridoids. Traditional body paint and food source. Limited supplement use; primarily in South American traditional preparations.
Hydrangea arborescens (Hydrangeaceae) root. Traditional Cherokee and Eclectic medicine remedy for kidney stones and urinary complaints. Contains hydrangin coumarin. Botanical root powder.
Hyssopus officinalis (Lamiaceae) aerial parts. Biblical herb containing pinocamphone ketone and marrubiin. Traditional expectorant and carminative. Used for respiratory and digestive complaints.

Inula racemosa (Asteraceae) root. Pushkarmoola in Ayurveda. Contains alantolactone and isoalantolactone sesquiterpene lactones. Traditional cardiotonic and respiratory herb. Distinct from I. helenium.
Pterocarpus marsupium (Fabaceae) heartwood/bark. Vijaysar in Ayurveda. Contains pterostilbene and epicatechin. Traditional anti-diabetic wood (water stored in kino wood cups). Botanical bark form.
Rubia cordifolia (Rubiaceae) root. Manjistha in Ayurveda. Contains purpurin and munjistin anthraquinones. Traditional blood-purifying and skin-clarifying herb. Botanical root powder.
Tinospora cordifolia (Menispermaceae) stem. Guduchi/Giloy in Ayurveda. Contains tinosporin, berberine, and polysaccharides. Rasayana herb for immune modulation and fever. Botanical stem powder.

Irvingia gabonensis (Irvingiaceae) seed kernel. African mango containing IGOB131 extract. RCT evidence for weight management (PMID 19254366). Contains soluble fiber and fatty acids. Botanical seed form.
Hedera helix (Araliaceae) leaf. English ivy containing alpha-hederin saponin. European HMPC-approved for productive cough in children and adults. Well-studied expectorant. Botanical leaf form.
Syzygium cumini (Myrtaceae) seed. Jamun in Ayurveda. Contains jamboline alkaloid and ellagic acid tannins. Traditional anti-diabetic remedy. Seed has higher bioactive concentration than fruit pulp.
Lonicera japonica (Caprifoliaceae) flower bud. Jin Yin Hua in TCM. Contains chlorogenic acid and luteolin. Heat-clearing and detoxifying herb. Major component of Yin Qiao San formula.

Reynoutria japonica (syn. Polygonum cuspidatum, Polygonaceae) root. Hu Zhang in TCM. Richest dietary source of trans-resveratrol (not standardized in botanical form). Also contains emodin anthraquinone.
Nardostachys jatamansi root/rhizome used in Ayurvedic formulations. Exact identity mapping for jatamasi label rows.
Orthosiphon stamineus (Lamiaceae) leaf. Cat's Whiskers tea. Contains sinensetin and eupatorin polymethoxyflavones. European traditional diuretic and renal herb. Botanical leaf form.
Helianthus tuberosus (Asteraceae) tuber. Rich in inulin (76% of dry weight), the highest natural source. Prebiotic fructo-oligosaccharide. Used in supplements as inulin source and GI support.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Jerusalem Artichoke | 1177 (Number of products:37)

Ziziphus jujuba (Rhamnaceae) fruit. Da Zao in TCM. Contains jujubosides, betulinic acid, and cAMP nucleotide. Traditional nervine and qi-tonifying fruit. Used for sleep and anxiety support.
Juniperus communis (Cupressaceae) berry (cone). Contains alpha-pinene and terpinen-4-ol in essential oil. Traditional diuretic and urinary antiseptic. Avoid in kidney disease. Botanical berry powder.
Brassica oleracea var. sabellica (Brassicaceae) leaf. Cruciferous vegetable rich in glucosinolates (glucobrassicin), lutein, zeaxanthin, and vitamin K. Used in green food supplement blends.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Kale | 2285 (Number of products:871)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Sceletium tortuosum (Aizoaceae) aerial parts. South African succulent (Kanna) containing mesembrine alkaloids. SRI (serotonin reuptake inhibitor) and PDE4 inhibitor activity. Traditional mood-elevating herb.

Trametes versicolor (Polyporaceae) fruiting body. Turkey tail mushroom (Japanese: Kawaratake). Contains PSK (polysaccharide-K) and PSP (polysaccharopeptide). Extensively studied in Japan for immune support.
Laminaria digitata / Ascophyllum nodosum (Laminariaceae) thallus. Brown seaweed naturally rich in iodine (1,500-8,000 µg/g). Contains fucoidan and alginate polysaccharides. Botanical seaweed powder.

Hygrophila auriculata identity mapping for kokilaksha Ayurvedic label rows.
Cola nitida (Malvaceae) seed. Contains caffeine (2-3.5%) and theobromine. Traditional West African stimulant. Used in supplements as natural caffeine source. Botanical seed powder.

Citrus limon (Rutaceae) fruit. Rich in citric acid, vitamin C, and limonene. Contains eriocitrin and hesperidin flavonoids in peel. Botanical fruit powder, not standardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Lemon | 77 (Number of products:520)

Citrus limon (Rutaceae) peel bioflavonoid complex. Contains eriocitrin, hesperidin, and diosmin. Extracted from lemon peel processing. Used for capillary integrity and antioxidant support.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Citrus Bioflavonoids | 21 (Number of products:165)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Citrus limon (Rutaceae) peel essential oil. Cold-pressed, containing D-limonene (60-70%), beta-pinene, and gamma-terpinene. Used in aromatherapy and as supplement flavoring. Botanical essential oil.
Cymbopogon citratus (Poaceae) aerial parts. Contains citral (geranial + neral, 65-85% of essential oil). Traditional carminative, febrifuge, and antimicrobial herb. Botanical herb powder.

Glycyrrhiza glabra (Fabaceae) root. Contains glycyrrhizin (200x sweeter than sucrose) and glabridin. Anti-inflammatory and adrenal support. Caution: glycyrrhizin causes pseudoaldosteronism at >50 mg/day.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Licorice | 33 (Number of products:1613)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ligustrum lucidum (Oleaceae) berry. Nü Zhen Zi in TCM. Contains oleanolic acid and ursolic acid triterpenes. Yin-nourishing herb for liver-kidney deficiency patterns. Botanical berry powder.
Citrus aurantiifolia (Rutaceae) fruit. Rich in vitamin C, citric acid, and flavonoids (hesperidin, naringenin). Botanical fruit powder, not standardized. Identity mapping for lime supplement labels.

Tilia europaea (Malvaceae) flower. Linden/lime blossom rich in mucilage, flavonoids (tiliroside), and volatile oils. European HMPC-approved for common cold symptoms. Traditional anxiolytic and diaphoretic.
Vaccinium vitis-idaea (Ericaceae) fruit. Scandinavian berry rich in proanthocyanidins and arbutin. Contains quercetin and resveratrol. Traditional Nordic food with antioxidant properties.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Lingonberry | 2636 (Number of products:18)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Hericium erinaceus (Hericiaceae) fruiting body. Contains hericenones and erinacines that stimulate NGF synthesis. RCT evidence for cognitive function (PMID 18844328). Botanical mushroom powder.
Symplocos racemosa, commonly called Lodhra or Lodhtree. Ayurvedic botanical used in women's health and astringent formulas.
Piper longum (Piperaceae) fruit. Contains piperine and piperlongumine. Pippali in Ayurveda, bioenhancer similar to black pepper. Traditional warming carminative. Botanical fruit powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Indian Long Pepper | 502 (Number of products:199)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Lepidium meyenii (Brassicaceae) root. Peruvian adaptogen grown at 4,000+ m altitude. Contains macamides, macaenes, and glucosinolates. RCT evidence for libido and fertility (PMID 19781622).

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Maca | 163 (Number of products:792)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Myristica fragrans aril/spice identity used for jatiphala label rows when DSLD groups the ingredient under mace.

botanical_ingredients.notes: Blend of Magnolia officinalis bark (honokiol, magnolol) and Phellodendron amurense bark (berberine). Relora® is a branded version. Used for cortisol modulation and stress. Botanical bark blend.
Grifola frondosa (Meripilaceae) fruiting body. Hen-of-the-woods mushroom. Contains maitake D-fraction beta-glucan. RCT evidence for immune modulation (PMID 19367670). Botanical mushroom powder.
Mangifera indica (Anacardiaceae) fruit. Rich in mangiferin xanthone, beta-carotene, and vitamin C. Tropical fruit used in supplement blends. Botanical fruit powder, not standardized.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|mango | 1085 (Number of products:211)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Mangifera indica (Anacardiaceae) leaf extract. Higher mangiferin content than fruit (up to 5% in young leaves). Traditional Ayurvedic remedy for diabetes. Botanical leaf form, unstandardized.
Garcinia mangostana (Clusiaceae) fruit pericarp. 'Queen of fruits' containing alpha-mangostin and gamma-mangostin xanthones. Potent antioxidant. Botanical pericarp powder, not standardized.

Leptospermum scoparium (Myrtaceae) nectar-derived honey. Contains methylglyoxal (MGO) as unique antimicrobial compound. UMF/MGO ratings measure antibacterial potency. New Zealand origin. Not a botanical per se.

aliases: maquibright aristotelia chilensis berry standardized extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Aristotelia chilensis (Elaeocarpaceae) berry. Chilean Patagonian berry with highest known anthocyanin content (138 mg/g freeze-dried). Contains delphinidins. Potent antioxidant. Botanical berry powder.
Althaea officinalis (Malvaceae) root. Contains mucilage polysaccharides (up to 35% dry weight). European HMPC-approved for dry cough and mild GI inflammation. Traditional demulcent herb.
Teramnus labialis, commonly transliterated as Mashaparni/Mashparnee in Ayurvedic formulas. Added for exact identity coverage from raw DSLD label text.
Camellia sinensis (Theaceae) stone-ground shade-grown leaf. Higher L-theanine and EGCG than standard green tea due to shade cultivation. Whole-leaf consumption provides more catechins than infusion.

Filipendula ulmaria (Rosaceae) aerial parts. Contains salicylaldehyde and salicin (natural aspirin precursors). European HMPC-approved for common cold and mild joint pain. Traditional anti-inflammatory.

Multi-species medicinal mushroom blend typically containing reishi, shiitake, maitake, turkey tail, and/or cordyceps. Identity mapping for generic mushroom blend labels. Composition varies by manufacturer.

Phellinus linteus (Hymenochaetaceae) fruiting body. Korean medicinal mushroom (Mesima/Sangwhang). Contains hispidin and beta-glucans. Studied for immune modulation and anti-tumor properties in Korean medicine.
Silybum marianum (Asteraceae) whole herb. Contains silymarin flavonolignan complex in seeds. Most-studied hepatoprotective botanical. This entry covers generic milk thistle herb, not seed-specific.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Milk Thistle | 652 (Number of products:1563)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Silybum marianum (Asteraceae) seed. Primary source of silymarin (silybin, silydianin, silychristin). RCT evidence for hepatoprotection and liver support (PMID 17127537). Botanical seed form.

botanical_ingredients.notes: Mimosa pudica (Fabaceae) seed/whole plant. Sensitive plant/touch-me-not. Contains mimosine and tannins. Used in Ayurveda for wound healing and as GI cleansing agent. Botanical seed/herb powder.
Moringa oleifera (Moringaceae) leaf. 'Miracle tree' containing isothiocyanates, quercetin, and chlorogenic acid. Exceptionally nutrient-dense (2x protein of yogurt, 4x calcium of milk per gram).
Leonurus cardiaca (Lamiaceae) aerial parts. Contains leonurine alkaloid and stachydrine. Traditional nervine and uterine tonic. European HMPC-approved for nervous heart complaints and mild anxiety.
Mucuna pruriens (Fabaceae) seed. Contains L-DOPA (3-6% in seeds), the direct dopamine precursor. Ayurvedic Kapikacchu for male fertility and Parkinson's symptoms (PMID 14737007). Botanical seed form.
Vigna trilobata, commonly transliterated as Mudgaparni/Mudgaparnee in Ayurvedic formulas. Added for exact identity coverage from raw DSLD label text.

Artemisia vulgaris (Asteraceae) aerial parts. Contains thujone (trace), flavonoids, and sesquiterpene lactones. Traditional European bitter tonic and emmenagogue. Used in moxibustion (TCM). Botanical herb.
Ptychopetalum olacoides (Olacaceae) bark/root. Amazonian 'potency wood.' Contains muirapuamine and lupeol triterpene. Traditional aphrodisiac and nervine tonic. Limited clinical evidence.
Verbascum thapsus (Scrophulariaceae) leaf. Contains mucilage, saponins (verbascosaponin), and iridoids. Traditional respiratory demulcent and expectorant. European HMPC-approved for cough.
Vigna radiata (Fabaceae) seed. Mung bean rich in vitexin and isovitexin flavonoids. Contains protease inhibitors and phytoestrogens. Used in supplements for anti-inflammatory and antioxidant support.

Urtica dioica (Urticaceae) leaf. Contains chlorophyll, histamine, formic acid, and flavonoids. European HMPC-approved for urinary complaints and joint pain. Nutritive mineral-rich herb.
Urtica dioica (Urticaceae) root. Distinct from leaf in bioactive profile — contains lectins, sterols, and lignans. European HMPC-approved for BPH symptoms. Botanical root form.
Nigella sativa (Ranunculaceae) seed. Black seed/black cumin containing thymoquinone (primary bioactive). RCT evidence for glycemic and lipid control (PMID 28068824). Botanical seed powder.
Vitex negundo, commonly called Nirgundi/Nirgundee or five-leaf chaste tree. Ayurvedic botanical used in classical joint and women's health formulas.
Morinda citrifolia (Rubiaceae) fruit. Noni juice rich in iridoids (deacetylasperulosidic acid) and scopoletin. Traditional Polynesian remedy. Novel food approved in EU (2003). Botanical fruit form.
Pyropia yezoensis (syn. Porphyra yezoensis, Bangiaceae) thallus. Japanese edible red seaweed. Rich in protein (25-50%), vitamin B12 (bioavailable form debated), and porphyran polysaccharide.

Ligusticum porteri (Apiaceae) root. Bear root, used by Native American and Hispanic traditional medicine for respiratory infections. Contains Z-ligustilide and phthalides. Conservation concern.
Paeonia lactiflora (Paeoniaceae) root. Bai Shao in TCM. Contains paeoniflorin monoterpene glycoside. Blood-nourishing and liver-softening herb. Used in Xiao Yao San and Si Wu Tang formulas.
Carica papaya (Caricaceae) fruit. Contains papain proteolytic enzyme, lycopene, and vitamin C. Traditional digestive aid. Green papaya has higher papain content than ripe fruit.

Passiflora incarnata (Passifloraceae) aerial parts. Same species as passion_flower entry; covers herb-specific labels. Contains benzoflavone chrysin and harman alkaloids. Botanical herb form.
Handroanthus impetiginosus (syn. Tabebuia avellanedae, Bignoniaceae) inner bark. Contains lapachol and beta-lapachone naphthoquinones. Traditional Amazonian antimicrobial. Botanical bark form.
Pisum sativum (Fabaceae) seed/shoot. Garden pea rich in plant protein, fiber, and saponins. Used in supplement blends for protein content and as vegetable powder. Botanical form.
Prunus persica (Rosaceae) fruit. Contains chlorogenic acid, catechin, and carotenoids. Traditional food with mild laxative properties. Used in supplement blends. Botanical fruit powder.

botanical_ingredients.notes: Pyrus communis (Rosaceae) fruit. Contains arbutin, chlorogenic acid, and dietary fiber. Hypoallergenic fruit often used in elimination diets. Botanical fruit powder in supplement blends.

Mentha × piperita (Lamiaceae) leaf. Contains menthol (30-55%) and menthone. RCT evidence for IBS (enteric-coated oil, PMID 17420159). European HMPC-approved for GI spasms. Botanical leaf form.

botanical_ingredients.notes: Perilla frutescens (Lamiaceae) leaf. Shiso/Zi Su Ye in TCM. Contains rosmarinic acid and luteolin. Anti-allergic properties via lipoxygenase inhibition. Traditional respiratory and digestive herb.
Phellodendron amurense (Rutaceae) bark. Huang Bai in TCM. Contains berberine (2-5%) and phellodendrine. Damp-heat clearing herb for lower jiao. Component of Er Miao San and Relora®.
Picrorhiza kurroa (Plantaginaceae) rhizome. Same species as picrorhiza_root; covers binomial labels. Contains picrosides I/II with hepatoprotective and immune-modulating activity.
Picrorhiza kurroa (Plantaginaceae) rhizome. Kutki in Ayurveda. Contains picrosides I and II (iridoid glycosides). Hepatoprotective herb. Used for liver and immune support. Botanical root form.
Plantago major (Plantaginaceae) leaf. Common plantain (not banana). Contains aucubin iridoid, mucilage, and allantoin. Traditional wound-healing and respiratory herb. Botanical leaf form.
Prunus domestica (Rosaceae) fruit. Contains chlorogenic acid, neochlorogenic acid, and sorbitol. Dried form (prune) has FDA-recognized laxative effects. Botanical fruit powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Plum | 1870 (Number of products:500)

Polypodium vulgare (Polypodiaceae) rhizome. Common polypody fern. Contains ecdysteroids and phytoecdysones. Traditional European expectorant and mild laxative. Botanical rhizome powder.
Punica granatum (Lythraceae) fruit. Rich in punicalagins, ellagitannins, and anthocyanins. RCT evidence for blood pressure and antioxidant status (PMID 22648174). Botanical fruit powder.

Solanum tuberosum (Solanaceae) tuber. Contains resistant starch, potassium, and protease inhibitors (PI2). Used in supplement blends. Note: green potato contains solanine glycoalkaloid.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Potato | 562 (Number of products:43)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Zanthoxylum americanum (Rutaceae) bark/berry. Northern prickly ash containing hydroxy-alpha-sanshool (numbing agent) and coumarins. Traditional Eclectic circulatory stimulant.
Prunus domestica (Rosaceae) dried fruit. Prune (dried plum) rich in sorbitol, chlorogenic acid, and boron. FDA-recognized laxative. RCT evidence for bone health (PMID 21681165). Botanical fruit.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Prune | 49 (Number of products:76)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Plantago ovata (Plantaginaceae) seed husk. Contains arabinoxylan mucilage fiber (85% soluble). FDA health claim for cholesterol reduction at 7g/day. Gold standard bulk-forming laxative.

Camellia sinensis var. assamica (Theaceae) post-fermented leaf. Microbially fermented tea containing theabrownins, gallic acid, and lovastatin (trace). Yunnan specialty with lipid-lowering properties.
Pueraria mirifica (Fabaceae) root. Thai herb containing miroestrol and deoxymiroestrol, potent phytoestrogens. Traditional use for menopausal symptoms. Caution: strong estrogenic activity.
Cucurbita pepo (Cucurbitaceae) fruit/seed. Rich in carotenoids (beta-carotene, lutein) and cucurbitins in seeds. Seed oil studied for BPH (PMID 26093535). Botanical fruit/seed powder.

Quassia amara (Simaroubaceae) wood. Most bitter natural substance known (quassin). Traditional antimalarial, digestive bitter, and antiparasitic. Contains quassinoids. Botanical wood chip form.
