botanical_ingredients.notes: Rubus idaeus (Rosaceae) seed. Rich in ellagic acid, alpha-linolenic acid, and vitamin E. Seed oil has favorable omega-3:omega-6 ratio. Botanical seed powder, not standardized.
Rauvolfia vomitoria (Apocynaceae) root bark. Contains reserpine and other indole alkaloids. Reserpine is an FDA-approved antihypertensive. Root bark used in African traditional medicine.
Rhodophyta division covering non-specific red algae/seaweed labels. May include Chondrus, Lithothamnion, or Palmaria species. Natural calcium and mineral source. Identity mapping only.
Trifolium pratense (Fabaceae) flower. Contains isoflavones (biochanin A, formononetin) with phytoestrogenic activity. RCT evidence for menopausal symptoms (PMID 16373244). Botanical flower form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Red Clover | 112 (Number of products:498)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Trifolium pratense (Fabaceae) flower. Same species as red_clover; covers flower-specific labels. Rich in isoflavones and coumestrol phytoestrogens. European HMPC traditional use registration.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Red Clover | 112 (Number of products:498)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Rubus idaeus (Rosaceae) fruit. Rich in ellagitannins, anthocyanins (cyanidin-3-sophoroside), and dietary fiber. Traditional use of leaf for uterine toning. Botanical whole-fruit powder.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Red Raspberry | 113 (Number of products:534)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ceanothus americanus (Rhamnaceae) root. New Jersey tea. Traditional Eclectic lymphatic remedy. Contains betulinic acid and ceanothine alkaloids. Used for spleen and lymph support.
Ganoderma lucidum (Ganodermataceae) fruiting body. Ling Zhi in TCM, 'mushroom of immortality.' Contains ganoderic acids (triterpenes) and beta-glucans. Adaptogenic and immune-modulating.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Reishi mushroom | 327 (Number of products:860)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Rhodiola rosea (Crassulaceae) root. Arctic adaptogen containing rosavins and salidroside. RCT evidence for fatigue reduction and stress resilience (PMID 22228617). Botanical root form.
Rhododendron caucasicum botanical identity used on Garden of Life labels as an extract disclosure. Maintained conservatively without assuming a narrower plant part than the label provides.
Rheum palmatum (Polygonaceae) root/rhizome. Da Huang in TCM. Contains sennosides and emodin anthraquinones. Stimulant laxative and blood-moving herb. Botanical root form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Rhubarb | 732 (Number of products:126)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Rosa canina (Rosaceae) fruit. Rich in vitamin C (426 mg/100g), galactolipid GOPO, and lycopene. RCT evidence for osteoarthritis (PMID 18326603). Traditional northern European remedy.
Ruta graveolens (Rutaceae) aerial parts. Contains rutin flavonoid (named after this plant), furanocoumarins, and acridone alkaloids. Traditional emmenagogue. Phototoxic — handle with care.

Chlorophytum borivilianum (Asparagaceae) root. Safed Musli in Ayurveda, traditional aphrodisiac and adaptogenic tonic. Contains saponins (furostanol glycosides). Used for vitality and reproductive health.
Salvia officinalis (Lamiaceae) leaf. Contains carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and thujone. RCT evidence for cognitive function and menopausal hot flashes (PMID 16988880). Botanical leaf form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Sage | 447 (Number of products:194)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ayurvedic common-name identity for Hemidesmus indicus, commonly called Sariva or Indian sarsaparilla. Added for exact Softgels label coverage.
Smilax ornata (Smilacaceae) root. Contains sarsasapogenin steroidal saponins. Traditional blood purifier and adaptogenic root. Used in Eclectic and Unani medicine. Botanical root powder.
Serenoa repens (Arecaceae) berry. Contains fatty acids and phytosterols with 5-alpha reductase inhibition. RCT evidence for BPH symptoms (PMID 16467543). Botanical berry/fruit form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Saw Palmetto | 121 (Number of products:1164)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Schisandra chinensis (Schisandraceae) fruit. Wu Wei Zi ('five flavor fruit') in TCM. Contains schisandrin and gomisin lignans. Adaptogenic hepatoprotective herb. Botanical berry form.
Schizonepeta tenuifolia (Lamiaceae) aerial parts. Jing Jie in TCM. Contains pulegone and menthone. Wind-cold releasing herb used for common cold and skin eruptions. Botanical herb form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Schizonepeta | 1960 (Number of products:124)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Hippophae rhamnoides (Elaeagnaceae) berry. Rich in omega-7 (palmitoleic acid, 16-22%), vitamin C (200-600 mg/100g), and carotenoids. Traditional Tibetan and Ayurvedic remedy.
Generic seaweed/marine algae covering non-specific seaweed supplement labels. May contain brown, red, or green macroalgae. Natural iodine source. Identity mapping only, not species-specific.
Sesbania grandiflora (Fabaceae) flower/leaf. Agati in Ayurveda. Contains saponins and flavonoids. Traditional remedy for headache and nasal catarrh. Limited supplement use.

Convolvulus pluricaulis (Convolvulaceae) whole plant. Ayurvedic medhya rasayana (brain tonic). Contains shankhpushpine alkaloids and flavonoids. Traditional remedy for anxiety and cognitive enhancement.
Asparagus racemosus (Asparagaceae) root. Shatavari in Ayurveda, premier female reproductive tonic. Contains shatavarins (steroidal saponins). Galactagogue and adaptogenic. Botanical root form.
Rumex acetosella (Polygonaceae) aerial parts. Contains anthraquinones and oxalic acid. Component of Essiac tea formula. Traditional folk remedy for cancer (limited evidence). Botanical herb form.
Lentinula edodes (Omphalotaceae) fruiting body. Contains lentinan beta-glucan (approved as adjunct cancer therapy in Japan). Rich in eritadenine. Culinary and medicinal mushroom.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|other|Shiitake Mushroom | 2634 (Number of products:321)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ligusticum chuanxiong (Apiaceae) rhizome. Chuan Xiong in TCM. Contains tetramethylpyrazine and ferulic acid. Blood-moving and wind-expelling herb. Component of Si Wu Tang formula.

Scutellaria lateriflora (Lamiaceae) aerial parts. American skullcap containing baicalin and scutellarein flavones. Traditional nervine for anxiety and insomnia. Distinct from Chinese skullcap (S. baicalensis).
Polygonatum multiflorum (Asparagaceae) rhizome. Yu Zhu in TCM. Contains polysaccharides, steroidal saponins, and lectins. Yin-nourishing herb for dryness patterns. Botanical root form.

aliases: japanese sophora sophora japonica flower bud extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Styphnolobium japonicum (syn. Sophora japonica, Fabaceae) flower bud. Huai Hua in TCM. Natural source of rutin bioflavonoid (up to 20% dry weight). Used for venous and capillary health.
Sorghum bicolor (Poaceae) bran. Red/black sorghum bran rich in 3-deoxyanthocyanidins (unique to sorghum), condensed tannins, and phenolic acids. Ancient grain bran for antioxidant support.
Arthrospira platensis (Spirulinaceae) whole cell. Blue-green cyanobacterium rich in protein (60-70%), C-phycocyanin, and gamma-linolenic acid. RCT evidence for lipid control (PMID 18855693).
Cucurbita maxima (Cucurbitaceae) fruit. Winter squash rich in beta-carotene, lutein, and zeaxanthin carotenoids. Used in whole-food supplement blends for antioxidant and vitamin A content.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Squash | 3640 (Number of products:13)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Mitchella repens (Rubiaceae) aerial parts. Partridge berry. Traditional Native American and Eclectic parturient herb. Contains saponins and tannins. Used for uterine preparation in late pregnancy.

aliases: St. John's wort aerial parts extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Hypericum perforatum (Hypericaceae) aerial parts. Contains hypericin and hyperforin. Extensive RCT evidence for mild-moderate depression (Cochrane). Drug interaction risk via CYP3A4 induction. Botanical form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|St. John's Wort | 1034 (Number of products:354)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Illicium verum (Schisandraceae) fruit. Contains trans-anethole (80-90%) and shikimic acid (industrial source for oseltamivir/Tamiflu synthesis). Caution: distinguish from toxic I. anisatum.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Star Anise | 1176 (Number of products:38)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Collinsonia canadensis (Lamiaceae) root. Traditional Eclectic medicine remedy for hemorrhoids and venous congestion. Contains rosmarinic acid and essential oil. Botanical root powder.
Fragaria × ananassa (Rosaceae) fruit. Rich in pelargonidin-3-glucoside anthocyanin, ellagic acid, and vitamin C (59 mg/100g). Botanical fruit powder used in supplement blends.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Strawberry | 400 (Number of products:810)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Pfaffia paniculata (Amaranthaceae) root. Brazilian ginseng (Para Toda). Contains pfaffic acid and pfaffosides (ecdysteroid-like saponins). Traditional adaptogenic tonic. Botanical root form.
Ipomoea batatas (Convolvulaceae) tuber. Orange varieties rich in beta-carotene (8,510 µg/100g). Purple varieties contain peonidin anthocyanins. Used in whole-food supplement blends.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Sweet Potato | 1243 (Number of products:69)

aliases: sweet wormwood (artemisia annua) aerial parts extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Artemisia annua (Asteraceae) aerial parts. Source of artemisinin antimalarial compound (Nobel Prize 2015). Contains artemisinin, artemisinic acid, and flavonoids. Traditional Qing Hao in TCM.
Artemisia annua leaf essential oil / volatile oil identity. Kept distinct from the broader sweet wormwood herb canonical so oil labels are not collapsed into whole-herb extract labels.
Citrus reticulata (Rutaceae) fruit/peel. Chen Pi (aged peel) in TCM. Rich in polymethoxyflavones (tangeretin, nobiletin) in peel. Traditional digestive and phlegm-resolving fruit.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|mandarin orange | 1084 (Number of products:39)

aliases: CherryPure Tart Cherry (Prunus cerasus) Proanthocyanidin extract
CherryPure Sour Cherry (Tart Cherry) Proanthocyanidin powder
CherryPure Sour Cherry (Tart Cherry) Proanthocyanidin extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Prunus cerasus (Rosaceae) fruit. Montmorency tart cherry rich in anthocyanins and melatonin. RCT evidence for post-exercise recovery, sleep, and uric acid reduction (PMID 23075559).

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Sour Cherry | 615 (Number of products:367)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Melaleuca alternifolia (Myrtaceae) leaf. Essential oil rich in terpinen-4-ol (30-48%) with broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity. Australian TGA-listed for minor skin infections. Topical use primarily.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Tea Tree Oil | 1049 (Number of products:1)

aliases: thyme thymus vulgaris whole plant oil extract
thyme thymus vulgaris leaf and stem oil extract
thyme (thymus vulgaris) leaf and stem oil extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Thymus vulgaris (Lamiaceae) aerial parts. Contains thymol and carvacrol phenolic monoterpenes. European HMPC-approved for productive cough. Traditional antimicrobial and expectorant herb.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Thyme | 1593 (Number of products:203)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Tillandsia usneoides (Bromeliaceae) aerial parts. Spanish moss, an epiphytic bromeliad. Traditional remedy for hemorrhoids and diabetes in Latin American folk medicine. Limited supplement use.
Solanum lycopersicum (Solanaceae) fruit. Primary dietary source of lycopene (3-5 mg/100g). Also contains naringenin and chlorogenic acid. Used in supplement blends for antioxidant content.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Tomato | 621 (Number of products:889)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Eurycoma longifolia (Simaroubaceae) root. Malaysian ginseng/Tongkat Ali. Contains eurycomanone and quassinoids. RCT evidence for testosterone and ergogenic effects (PMID 23754792). Botanical root form.

aliases: Toothed Clubmoss (Huperzia serrata) aerial parts extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Huperzia serrata (Lycopodiaceae) whole plant. Source of huperzine A, a potent reversible AChE inhibitor. Chinese club moss (Qian Ceng Ta). Botanical whole-plant form, not purified huperzine.
Paeonia suffruticosa (Paeoniaceae) root bark. Mu Dan Pi in TCM. Contains paeonol phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory activity. Blood-cooling and stasis-removing herb. Botanical bark form.

Tribulus terrestris (Zygophyllaceae) fruit/aerial parts. Contains protodioscin steroidal saponin. Marketed for testosterone support; RCT evidence does not consistently support this claim. Botanical form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Tribulus | 2012 (Number of products:451)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ayurvedic formula of three fruits: Amalaki (Phyllanthus emblica), Bibhitaki (Terminalia bellirica), Haritaki (Terminalia chebula). Traditional rasayana and GI tonic. Equal parts blend.

Rheum palmatum (Polygonaceae) root. Turkey rhubarb variant of Chinese rhubarb. Contains anthraquinone glycosides (sennoside A/B, rhein, emodin). Traditional stimulant laxative. Component of Essiac tea.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|botanical|Chinese Rhubarb | 2469 (Number of products:163)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Curcuma longa (Zingiberaceae) rhizome. Contains curcuminoids (curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, bisdemethoxycurcumin, 2-5%) and turmerone essential oil. Most-studied botanical anti-inflammatory.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Turmeric | 48 (Number of products:2583)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Curcuma longa (Zingiberaceae) root powder. Same species as turmeric; covers powder-specific labels. Whole rhizome powder with low curcuminoid bioavailability (needs piperine or lipid co-administration).

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Turmeric | 48 (Number of products:2583)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Tylophora indica (Apocynaceae) leaf. Contains tylophorine and tylophorinine alkaloids. Traditional Ayurvedic remedy for asthma and allergies. RCT evidence for bronchial asthma (PMID 18288292).
Arctostaphylos uva-ursi (Ericaceae) leaf. Same species as uva_ursi_leaf; covers generic label variants. Contains arbutin urinary antiseptic. European HMPC-approved for uncomplicated UTI.
Arctostaphylos uva-ursi (Ericaceae) leaf. Contains arbutin (hydroquinone glycoside, 5-12%) and tannins. European HMPC-approved for uncomplicated UTI. Traditional urinary antiseptic herb.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Uva Ursi | 130 (Number of products:528)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Valeriana officinalis (Caprifoliaceae) root. Contains valerenic acid and iridoids (valepotriates). European HMPC-approved for sleep and mild anxiety. RCT evidence for insomnia (PMID 16619700).

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Valerian | 131 (Number of products:679)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Generic multi-vegetable powder blend covering non-specific vegetable supplement labels. Composition varies by manufacturer. Identity mapping only. Not attributable to single species.
Vitex agnus-castus (Lamiaceae) berry. Same species as chaste_tree; covers generic vitex labels. Contains diterpenes acting on dopamine D2 receptors. Traditional female reproductive herb.
Undaria pinnatifida (Alariaceae) thallus. Japanese edible brown seaweed containing fucoxanthin carotenoid and fucoidan polysaccharide. RCT evidence for metabolic support (PMID 19840063).

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|other|Wakame | 2443 (Number of products:23)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Eutrema japonicum (syn. Wasabia japonica, Brassicaceae) rhizome. Contains 6-MSITC (6-methylsulfinylhexyl isothiocyanate). Potent antimicrobial. Most commercial wasabi is horseradish substitute.
Nasturtium officinale (Brassicaceae) aerial parts. Cruciferous herb rich in PEITC (phenethyl isothiocyanate) and vitamin K. Traditional blood purifier and respiratory herb. Botanical herb form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Watercress | 723 (Number of products:307)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Citrullus lanatus (Cucurbitaceae) fruit. Richest food source of L-citrulline (amino acid, 250 mg/100g). Also contains lycopene. Used in supplements for nitric oxide support.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Watermelon | 727 (Number of products:144)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Triticum aestivum (Poaceae) germ. Embryo portion rich in vitamin E (tocopherols), octacosanol, and policosanol. Contains wheat germ agglutinin lectin. Not suitable for celiac/gluten-sensitive.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Wheat germ | 1639 (Number of products:20)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Blend of Triticum aestivum (wheat) and Hordeum vulgare (barley) young grass. Alkalizing green food supplement rich in chlorophyll, SOD, and trace minerals. Harvested before jointing (gluten-free).
Triticum aestivum (Poaceae) young grass. Harvested before jointing stage. Rich in chlorophyll, SOD enzyme, and vitamins. Popularized by Ann Wigmore. Gluten-free when harvested at grass stage.
Vitis vinifera (Vitaceae) white/green skinned fruit. Lower anthocyanin but rich in stilbenes, flavanols, and hydroxycinnamates. Used in supplement blends. Botanical fruit powder.
Morus alba (Moraceae) leaf. Contains 1-deoxynojirimycin (DNJ) alpha-glucosidase inhibitor. Traditional remedy for glycemic management in TCM and Unani medicine. Botanical leaf form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|White Mulberry | 1878 (Number of products:86)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Quercus alba (Fagaceae) bark. Rich in tannins (6-11%, mostly ellagitannins). Traditional astringent for diarrhea, sore throat, and hemorrhoids. European HMPC traditional use. Botanical bark powder.
Santalum album identity for sandalwood label rows that DSLD classifies as White Sandalwood botanical.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|White Sandalwood | 2006 (Number of products:6)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Salix alba (Salicaceae) bark. Contains salicin (natural aspirin precursor, 1-2%). European HMPC-approved for low back pain and mild fever. RCT evidence for osteoarthritis (PMID 11201053).
Vaccinium angustifolium (Ericaceae) fruit. Lowbush wild blueberry with 2x anthocyanin content vs. cultivated. Rich in pterostilbene and chlorogenic acid. Superior antioxidant capacity.
Baptisia tinctoria (Fabaceae) root. Contains isoflavones and polysaccharides. Traditional Eclectic immune stimulant. Component of Esberitox® with Echinacea and Thuja. Botanical root form.

Lactuca virosa (Asteraceae) leaf/latex. Contains lactucin and lactucopicrin sesquiterpene lactones with mild sedative properties. Traditional pain-relieving herb ('lettuce opium'). Botanical leaf form.
Dioscorea villosa (Dioscoreaceae) root. Contains diosgenin steroidal saponin. Traditional antispasmodic. Note: diosgenin does NOT convert to progesterone in vivo. Botanical root form.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Wild Yam | 646 (Number of products:356)

aliases: cqr-300 cissus quadrangularis stem and leaf extract
botanical_ingredients.notes: Cissus quadrangularis (Vitaceae) stem. Hadjod in Ayurveda ('bone setter'). Contains 3-ketosteroids and quadrangularin C. Traditional remedy for bone fractures and joint health.

aliases: Wintergreen (Gaultheria procumbens) extract salicyllic acid derivatives
botanical_ingredients.notes: Gaultheria procumbens (Ericaceae) leaf. Contains methyl salicylate (up to 98% of essential oil). Natural aspirin analog. Caution: methyl salicylate toxicity risk at high doses. Botanical leaf form.
Hamamelis virginiana (Hamamelidaceae) leaf/bark. Rich in hamamelitannin and gallic acid tannins (8-12%). Traditional astringent for hemorrhoids, varicose veins, and skin inflammation.
Isatis tinctoria (Brassicaceae) root. Ban Lan Gen in TCM. Contains indirubin and tryptanthrin. Traditional antiviral and heat-clearing herb. Historical European dye plant (indigo source).
Stachys officinalis (syn. Betonica officinalis, Lamiaceae) aerial parts. Contains stachydrine, betonicine, and tannins. Traditional European nervine, cephalic, and digestive herb.
Artemisia absinthium (Asteraceae) leaf. Contains absinthin bitter sesquiterpene and thujone (neurotoxic at high doses). Traditional bitter digestive tonic. Component of absinthe spirit.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Wormwood | 425 (Number of products:113)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Xanthoparmelia scabrosa (Parmeliaceae) thallus. Lichen marketed for male sexual function enhancement. Contains salazinic acid. Very limited clinical evidence; safety profile poorly characterized.
Smallanthus sonchifolius (Asteraceae) root. Andean tuber rich in fructo-oligosaccharides (FOS, 40-70% dry weight). Natural prebiotic with low glycemic index. Used for glycemic and gut health support.
Achillea millefolium (Asteraceae) aerial parts. Contains chamazulene, achillein alkaloid, and flavonoids. European HMPC-approved for minor wounds and digestive complaints. Traditional styptic herb.
Rumex crispus (Polygonaceae) root. Contains anthraquinone glycosides (chrysophanol, emodin) and iron-rich tannins. Traditional alterative for iron-deficiency anemia and skin conditions.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Yellow Dock | 510 (Number of products:275)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Ilex paraguariensis (Aquifoliaceae) leaf. South American stimulant containing caffeine (1-2%), theobromine, and chlorogenic acids. Rich in saponins (matesaponins). Traditional social beverage.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Mate | 1559 (Number of products:139)

botanical_ingredients.notes: Yucca schidigera (Asparagaceae) root/stem. Mojave yucca containing steroidal saponins (sarsasapogenin, smilagenin). Traditional anti-arthritic and anti-inflammatory. Used in pet supplements too.
Citrus junos (Rutaceae) fruit. Japanese citrus rich in limonene, linalool, and naringin flavonoid. Contains hesperidin and nomilin limonoid. Used in aromatherapy and as vitamin C source.
Polyporus umbellatus (Polyporaceae) sclerotium. Zhu Ling in TCM, diuretic mushroom. Contains ergosterol and polysaccharides (beta-glucans). Traditional remedy for edema and urinary difficulty.

Smilax china L. (Smilacaceae) root — traditional Ayurvedic (madhusnuhi) and Chinese medicine (tu fuling) botanical. Contains steroidal saponins (sarsasaponin, smilasaponin), flavonoids, stilbenes, and polyphenols. Traditional uses: anti-inflammatory, diuretic, blood purification, rheumatoid arthritis support, and skin conditions (psoriasis, eczema). Distinct from Smilax ornata (sarsaparilla) and from Smilax regelii. Clinical evidence sparse — primarily in vitro and animal studies. High doses may cause GI irritation (saponin content). No FDA safety alerts.
Cucurbita pepo (Cucurbitaceae) fruit. Nutrient-rich vegetable powder used in greens blends. No therapeutic scoring rationale.
Cicer arietinum (Fabaceae) seed powder. Plant protein source in superfood blends.
Phaseolus vulgaris (Fabaceae) black variety. Plant protein/fiber source in blends.
Artemisia dracunculus (Asteraceae) herb. Some evidence for glucose management (PMID 22034519). Used in GNC sports nutrition products.
Melilotus officinalis (Fabaceae). Contains coumarin and melilotin. Traditional use for venous insufficiency. Only 1 GNC occurrence.

Chenopodium quinoa — pseudocereal grain. Complete protein with all 9 essential amino acids. Rich in manganese, phosphorus, magnesium, folate. Gluten-free. Commonly featured in whole-food supplement blends.
Vigna angularis — East Asian legume, rich in protein, fiber, and flavonoids.
Amaranthus spp. — pseudocereal grain, gluten-free complete protein. Distinct from FD&C Red #2 artificial dye (also named "amaranth").
Khorasan wheat — ancient wheat variety. Higher protein and selenium than common wheat. Contains gluten.
Murraya koenigii — Indian aromatic herb. Traditional uses for digestion; antioxidant compounds.
Hemp seed protein — complete plant protein, ~50% protein by weight, rich in arginine.
Piper betle leaf — traditional Ayurvedic botanical. Modern formulas use leaf extract (not betel quid with areca).
Vigna unguiculata / Dolichos biflorus — Ayurvedic legume extract used for kidney/urinary support.
Pleurotus eryngii — gourmet edible mushroom with beta-glucan content.
Rubus occidentalis — distinct from red raspberry. Rich in anthocyanins and ellagitannins.
Laminaria japonica / Saccharina japonica — edible brown seaweed. Richest natural iodine source.
Navy bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) — small white bean, common in whole-food supplement matrices. Rich in protein, fiber, folate.

aliases: Essence of organic Lavandin (flower & stem) oil
botanical_ingredients.notes: Lavandula x intermedia (Lavandin) — hybrid of true lavender and spike lavender. Essential oil used for fragrance and aromatherapy.
Plukenetia volubilis — Amazonian vine producing edible seeds. Richest plant source of omega-3 ALA (~50% of seed oil), plus protein and tocopherols.
Lantana camara — traditional South Asian medicinal plant. Caution: known phototoxicity; supplement-grade use limited.
Wrightia tinctoria — Ayurvedic botanical from India. Traditional uses for skin conditions.
Brassica oleracea var. acephala — cruciferous leafy green. Vitamin K, A, C source; glucosinolate content.
Citrus sinensis (Sweet Orange) — flavor/aromatic botanical. Peel contains limonene; fruit vitamin C and bioflavonoids.

Cynanchum wilfordii (Apocynaceae) root. Korean traditional herb used in women's health / menopause formulations (component of Estromon). Contains cynandione A, gagaminine, acetophenones. IMPORTANT: adulteration risk — historically confused/substituted with Polygonum multiflorum (He Shou Wu, hepatotoxic) on US market. Verify binomial on label.

Phlomoides umbrosa (Lamiaceae; formerly Phlomis umbrosa) root. Korean traditional herb, component of Estromon women's-health formulation. Contains iridoid glycosides (shanzhiside methyl ester, sesamoside, phlomiol). Note: Korean vernacular 'Sok-Dan' overlaps with TCM Xu Duan (Dipsacus) — verify binomial, not common name.

botanical_ingredients.cui_verified_source: UMLS (pre-verified in existing pipeline; Tamarindus indica concept C0965568)
botanical_ingredients.notes: Tamarindus indica fruit — legume-family tropical fruit used for dietary fiber and polyphenols. Source of tartaric acid and malic acid. Used in gummies and chewables as natural flavor + fiber source.
Green Bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) — edible legume used in whole-food supplement blends for plant protein + fiber.
Lima Bean (Phaseolus lunatus) — edible legume used in whole-food supplement blends for plant protein + fiber.
botanical_ingredients.cui_note: No confirmed single UMLS concept for Phaseolus lunatus at supplement-ingredient level; track governed-null per protocol.
No confirmed single UMLS concept for Ecklonia radiata; marine kelp species, tracked governed-null per protocol.
